Exclusive: Jewelry startup Giva set to raise $28 Mn debt
results.description: Omnichannel jewelry startup Giva is set to raise Rs 270 crore ($28 million) through a multi-tranche debt round, led by BlackSoil Capital, with participation from InCred Credit Fund, Stride Ventures, and Nuvama Crossover Yield Opportunities Fund. The company is raising debt through multiple debenture issuances. This includes Rs 90 crore from BlackSoil Capital, Rs 70 crore from InCred Credit Fund, Rs 40 crore from Nuvama Crossover Yield Opportunities, and Rs 70 crore from Stride Ventures, accoirding the filing reviewed by Entrackr. As per the filing, the debt has a fixed rate of interest of 13.4% per annum which will be payable on monthly basis. The proceeds from the proposed debt round will be used towards working capital requirements, capital expenditure for setting up stores, and other general corporate purposes, filings added. Founded in 2019, Giva originally launched as an affordable jewelry brand, has expanded into gold jewelry and lab-grown diamonds. Led by Ishendra Agarwal, it operates around 210 physical stores across 25 cities in India, alongside its website and app. It adopted a franchise-led model to expand. Last year, the firm indicated that it would close FY26 with more than 300 Giva outlets across the country. Giva has raised over $158 million to date, including its Rs 110 crore (around $12 million) in its Series C extension round led by HPV CC1 ltd in February this year. The new debt could be a part of larger equity and debt funding. While the company is yet to file its FY26 numbers, for the fiscal year ending March 2025, Giva reported a substantial 89% increase in operating revenue, which climbed to Rs 518 crore. Unlike its scale, the company’s losses grew only 22% year-on-year to Rs 72 crore in the same period. GIVA competes with large organised jewellery players such as BlueStone, CaratLane, Melorra, Candere, and Tanishq, along with new-age brands including Palmonas, Jewelbox, Limelight Lab Grown Diamonds, Fiona Diamonds, and Firefly Diamonds.

results.title: Meta Layoffs 2026: Severance Package For Over 8,000 Employees Explained
results.description: If an employee finds and accepts a new role within Meta itself no later than one week before their termination date, the agreement becomes void and they receive no severance.

results.title: Saudi Arabia’s construction cost index rises 2.4% in April: GASTAT
results.description: article author: Arab News Author: Thu, 2026-05-21 10:40 RIYADH: Saudi Arabia’s Construction Cost Index rose 2.4 percent year on year in April, driven by higher equipment rental and labor costs, with both residential and non-residential sectors recording increases, official data showed. According to the General Authority for Statistics, residential construction costs climbed 2.4 percent annually, while the non-residential sector witnessed a price rise of 2.7 percent. Main category: Business & Economy Tags: construction Construction Cost Index General Authority for Statistics (GASTAT)
